title: Skeleton format spec hints do not match Pantry actual output sections
description: The skeleton templates have format spec hints that do not exactly match the sections the Pantry actually writes. dirt-pusher-skeleton.md (line 30) says 'Sections: Context, Scope Boundaries, Focus' but the Pantry also writes a 'Summary Doc Sections' section. nitpicker-skeleton.md (line 19) says 'Sections: Scope, Files, Focus, Detailed Instructions' but the Pantry writes sections including commit range, file list, report output path, bead-filing prohibition, and messaging guidelines. Additionally, big-head-skeleton.md has no format hint at all, unlike the other two skeletons. Affected surfaces: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md:30, nitpicker-skeleton.md:19, big-head-skeleton.md:53. Fix: Update skeleton hints to match Pantry output, or add '(approximate)' qualifier. Add format hint to big-head-skeleton.md.

title: Dual placeholder conventions for round-conditional blocks
description: Root cause: pantry.md uses {PANTRY_ROUND_1_CHECK_START/END} braces while reviews.md uses <IF ROUND 1> angle brackets for the same conditional construct. Risk of unresolved markers in composed briefs. Affected files: orchestration/templates/pantry.md:382-416,399-419 and orchestration/templates/reviews.md:466-545. Fix: Standardize on one marker convention. Add Pantry self-check for leftover markers. Acceptance criteria: (1) One consistent marker convention. (2) Pantry validates composed briefs for leftover markers. Found by: Excellence (EX-1, EX-5 both P3).

title: Pantry guard edge cases: whitespace handling and contamination detection carve-out
TOCTOU race: Pantry fail-fast metadata check may read partially-written Scout file
pantry-review.md (archived) tense inconsistency
Pantry 'report to Queen immediately' misleading for Task subagent model
pantry.md Section 2 deprecation notice insufficiently prominent
pantry.md fail-fast conditions use inconsistent signal words
Pantry empty file list guard does not validate that listed files exist on disk
description: The Pantry empty file list guard (pantry.md:217-228) checks if the Queen-provided file list is empty or whitespace-only, but does not validate that the listed files actually exist on disk. If git state changes between file list generation and Pantry execution (e.g., git reset, branch switch), the Pantry would compose review briefs referencing nonexistent files. This is extremely low likelihood since the Pantry runs immediately after list generation. Affected surfaces: pantry.md:217-228. Fix: Low priority. Optionally add a note: 'The Pantry trusts the Queen file list as authoritative. File existence is verified by Nitpickers at review time.'

title: pantry.md Section 2 deprecation notice insufficiently prominent
Failure taxonomy (INFRASTRUCTURE vs SUBSTANCE) not defined in a shared location
description: The orchestration templates use two failure labels -- INFRASTRUCTURE FAILURE and SUBSTANCE FAILURE -- to distinguish failure root causes. These labels appear across multiple files (pantry.md, big-head-skeleton.md, checkpoints.md, reviews.md) but are never formally defined in one canonical location. A reader encountering one label in isolation has no reference to understand the full taxonomy. Affected surfaces: pantry.md:33/45, big-head-skeleton.md:58, checkpoints.md:333, reviews.md. Fix: Add a brief taxonomy definition in a shared location (e.g., checkpoints.md or a new reference doc) and reference it from each template.

title: Pantry partial verdict table placement ambiguous -- per-failure or end-of-loop
description: The 'On any failure above' block in pantry.md (lines 68-74) with the partial verdict table appears after Condition 3 at the same indentation level. It is ambiguous whether this block runs once after processing ALL tasks (intended behavior) or after EACH individual failure (mid-loop). The placement after Condition 3 could be read as specific to that condition rather than a general end-of-loop instruction. Affected surfaces: pantry.md:68-74. Fix: Move the partial verdict table instruction to after the per-task loop ends and clarify: 'After processing all tasks, if any failures occurred, return a single partial verdict table.'

title: pantry.md fail-fast conditions use inconsistent signal words
Incomplete failure paths in agent protocols: Big Head escalation, Nitpicker missing brief, Pantry error handling
Pantry fail-fast check uses redundant 'missing, does not exist' phrasing
fix: remove archived pantry-review from scout.md exclusion list
description:
orchestration/templates/scout.md:63 lists `pantry-review` in the agent exclusion list: "scout-organizer, pantry-impl, pantry-review, pest-control, nitpicker, big-head". The pantry-review agent is archived (orchestration/_archive/pantry-review.md) and has no file in agents/.

GLOSSARY.md correctly uses strikethrough for pantry-review. pantry.md correctly warns against spawning it. Only the scout.md exclusion list still references it as if it were active.

Since pantry-review has no agent file in agents/, it would not appear in the Scout's catalog anyway — the reference is harmless but signals the list was not updated on deprecation.

Fix: Remove `pantry-review` from the exclusion list in scout.md:63.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] scout.md exclusion list no longer references pantry-review

Audit reference: DRIFT-021 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Pantry failure artifact path collision across conditions for same task
description: All three Pantry failure conditions (Condition 1: missing files, Condition 2: incomplete metadata, Condition 3: placeholder contamination) write their failure artifacts to the same path: {session-dir}/prompts/task-{TASK_SUFFIX}-FAILED.md. While only one condition fires per task in the current sequential waterfall, the shared path means: (1) the failure type is only distinguishable by reading the artifact content, not the filename; (2) a future refactoring that makes checks non-sequential could cause silent overwrites. Affected surfaces: pantry.md:33, pantry.md:47, pantry.md:58. Fix: Low priority. Add a comment documenting the sequential-check invariant. Optionally add condition type to filename.

title: pantry-review.md self-validation checklist lacks remediation guidance per item
pantry.md references undefined 'two-script approach'
description:
Root cause: pantry.md:238-243 Section 2 deprecation notice says 'superseded by the two-script approach introduced in Section 1 Step 2.5' without naming the scripts. The term 'two-script approach' appears only once in the file with no definition or link.

Affected surfaces:
- orchestration/templates/pantry.md:238-243

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F7). Single reviewer, standalone finding.

Fix: Add script names parenthetically: 'the two-script approach (compose-review-skeletons.sh + fill-review-slots.sh)'.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Deprecation notice names both scripts explicitly

title: pantry-review.md (archived) tense inconsistency
Pantry fail-fast says Halt but behavior is skip-to-next-task
description: Line 30 of pantry.md says 'FAIL-FAST CHECK: Halt and report for any of these conditions.' The word 'Halt' implies stopping all processing. But the actual behavior in Conditions 1-3 is per-task skipping: 'Do not proceed with task brief composition for this task' and 'skip to the next task.' The Pantry continues processing remaining tasks after a failure. The 'Halt' wording became more visibly misleading after ant-farm-zeu added detailed per-condition behavior (write artifact, skip to next). Affected surfaces: pantry.md:30-31. Fix: Change 'Halt and report' to 'Check and skip on failure' or 'Validate before proceeding.'

title: Pantry review mode has no fail-fast for empty changed-file list
